goal: Add workspace directory validation before job execution in orchestrator Start/StartAsync and MCP start-job handling, then verify with build and tests.
tech_stack: Go 1.26, standard library, MCP JSON-RPC server
summary: Implement a small preflight validation path for job creation so invalid workspace directories are rejected before persistence or background execution. Update internal/orchestrator/service.go so Start and StartAsync validate the resolved job.WorkspaceDir immediately after job construction and return a clear error when the path is invalid. Mirror that validation in internal/mcp/server.go toolStartJob so MCP callers get a friendly failure before StartAsync is invoked. Add regression coverage for service and MCP behavior, update the relevant docs to reflect the new validation, and finish by running go build ./... and go test ./...
product_scope: Pre-run workspace directory validation in internal/orchestrator/service.go Start
Pre-run workspace directory validation in internal/orchestrator/service.go StartAsync
MCP gorechera_start_job validation in internal/mcp/server.go before StartAsync
Regression tests for invalid workspace_dir handling
Relevant docs update for the new validation behavior
Repository verification with go build ./... and go test ./...
non_goals: Changing workspace fallback semantics based on workspaceRoot
Changing evaluator, approval, or runLoop behavior beyond early validation
Adding broader filesystem validation beyond this workspace_dir existence check
Changing unrelated MCP tools or job lifecycle APIs
Skipping required build and test verification
proposed_steps: Inspect the existing Start, StartAsync, and toolStartJob flows to identify the exact post-job-construction validation point and current MCP error surfacing behavior.

Add a small shared validation helper in internal/orchestrator/service.go or equivalent local logic that checks the resolved job.WorkspaceDir when non-empty with os.Stat and returns a clear error such as "workspace directory does not exist: {path}" before SaveJob or runLoop.
Apply that validation in both Start and StartAsync after the job struct is created and before any persistence or goroutine launch occurs.
Update internal/mcp/server.go toolStartJob to validate workspace_dir before calling StartAsync and return a user-friendly MCP-facing error for invalid input.
Add or extend tests in internal/orchestrator/service_test.go to cover Start and StartAsync rejecting a nonexistent workspace directory and preserving normal behavior for valid paths.
Add MCP regression coverage, likely via a new internal/mcp/server_test.go, to verify gorechera_start_job rejects an invalid workspace_dir with a friendly error message.
Update docs under docs/ to note that job creation now fails fast when workspace_dir is invalid.
Run go build ./... and go test ./... and use the results as the final verification gate.
success_signals: Starting a job with a missing workspace path fails immediately with a clear path-specific error.
No background goroutine or persisted job is created for an invalid workspace_dir.
MCP callers see a readable validation failure for invalid workspace_dir instead of a generic downstream failure.
Existing valid job creation flows continue to work unchanged.
Repository-wide build and test commands pass after the change.

verification_contract.goal: Verify that invalid workspace directories are rejected at job creation time in orchestrator and MCP paths, with passing build and test suites.
scope: Regression tests for service and MCP behavior
required_checks: Confirm Start rejects nonexistent workspace_dir before SaveJob/runLoop.
Confirm StartAsync rejects nonexistent workspace_dir before SaveJob/goroutine launch.
Confirm error text includes the invalid path.
Confirm toolStartJob rejects invalid workspace_dir before StartAsync and returns a friendly MCP-visible error.
Confirm valid workspace paths still allow normal job creation.
disallowed_actions: Do not bypass existing job lifecycle semantics beyond early validation.
Do not change workspace fallback behavior unless required by the validation implementation.
Do not skip repository-wide build and test verification.
verification_contract.notes: Keep the change narrowly scoped to fail-fast workspace validation and matching regression coverage.
